prompt: Design a MedRights Patient Media & Consent Portal to let a clinic manage patient master profiles while protecting the originality and authorized reuse of patient-facing artwork and clinical visuals in a fully offline environment. Users include Front Desk Staff who register and maintain care-recipient profiles, Clinicians who view and attach approved visuals to patient materials, Compliance Officers who review access and disputes, and Administrators who control policies and perform archival/export. The React web UI provides local username-and-password sign-in with optional “remember this device” for 30 days, plus a guest switcher for shared workstations that keeps separate recent-patient lists. Staff can search or create an MPI record, view masked identifiers by default, and request a time-bound patient consent to share or export selected data; consent screens must show purpose, expiration date, and a one-click revocation option. When staff upload artwork or handout images, the interface offers optional watermarking controls (clinic name, date stamp, and opacity defaulting to 35%) and shows an originality status banner (original, reposted with citation, or disputed). Repost flows require a citation statement and an attached authorization record before the image can be used in external-facing documents. Infringement reporting is available to Compliance Officers, capturing a local screenshot file, a typed URL or reference, and notes, then tracking dispute resolution states with visible timestamps. Report subscriptions allow users to schedule daily/weekly snapshots to be generated as PDF/image/Excel and saved to a local “Outbox” for on-prem delivery (print queue or shared folder) with clear failure badges, retries, and a runtime dashboard.The backend uses Django to enforce business rules, process files, and expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend, with MySQL as the system of record for MPI, consent, authorization, disputes, subscriptions, and audit trails. All authentication is local: passwords are hashed (Argon2), sessions expire after 15 minutes idle (8 hours absolute), and administrative actions (user disabling, bulk export, log purge) require a second confirmation plus re-entering the password. Patient identifiers are stored encrypted at rest (AES-256 via application-managed keys) and displayed masked unless a “break-glass” justification is entered, which is always logged and reviewable. Each uploaded image is fingerprinted using a local cryptographic hash and stored with immutable evidence metadata; watermarking is applied server-side so the saved derivative is traceable. Abnormal behavior rules include throttling to 5 failed logins per 10 minutes per workstation and automatic blacklisting after 3 lockouts in 24 hours, with an admin-only unblock workflow. Audit logging captures logins, CRUD, exports, approvals, key-field changes, and dispute actions; logs are searchable for 180 days and then archived locally for 7 years with tamper-evident chaining. Payment and reconciliation are implemented only as an offline, manual posting interface: staff can create a payable order, record a cash/check payment, close unpaid orders after 30 minutes, record refunds with status tracking, and export a daily reconciliation file for the accounting system, with all adjustments fully audited and compensating entries required instead of deletion.
